note: Tempdoc 602 R6 — the per-query search-trace DEGRADATION reason vocabulary is ONE closed authority, the search-side sibling of readiness-reason-codes.v1.json (NOT merged with it — two vocabularies, per tempdoc 600 PART IX). The producer is the worker `SearchReasonCode` enum; its `.name()` wire strings populate the three user-tier degradation fields (vectorBlockedReason / hybridFallbackReason / spladeSkipReason, all carried by VectorEncoding.Failed / SpladeEncoding.Failed). The FE `DEGRADATION_REASON_WORDING` map (searchTraceExplain.ts) words them. The gate (scripts/ci/check-search-degradation-reason-codes.mjs) enforces correspondence so neither drifts: FORWARD = every non-exempt SearchReasonCode member is worded (so a degraded search-explain line can never show the raw `(CODE)` to the user — the Nielsen 'no error codes' invariant); BACKWARD = every wording key is a real enum member or a declared feDerived one (no dead/typo rows). The noWordingExempt list is the only curated artifact: the 11 chunk-merge codes are emitted by the chunk-merge STAGE reason (a diagnostic-tier facet rendered raw per the 577 altitude cut), never by the three user-tier degradation fields, so wording them would be dead UI.
